h2h_summary: Morocco and Haiti have never previously met in any official international match, making their 2026 World Cup group stage encounter their first-ever fixture.
wc_history_home: Morocco made history at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar by becoming the first African and Arab nation to reach the semi-finals, finishing fourth after stunning wins over Spain and Portugal.
wc_history_away: Haiti's only previous World Cup appearance was in 1974 in West Germany, where they famously had Emmanuel Sanon end Dino Zoff's record scoreless streak before losing all three group games; their 2026 appearance ends a 52-year absence.
group_context: Group C features five-time world champions Brazil alongside Africa's top-ranked side Morocco (7th in the world), returning giant-killers Scotland, and debutant Haiti — Morocco and Haiti meet on Matchday 3, with both teams needing points to secure or consolidate a knockout-stage berth.
strengths_home: World-class individual talent led by Achraf Hakimi and Brahim Diaz
Elite defensive organisation and transition play built over years under Regragui and now Ouahbi
Strong tournament pedigree and mental resilience from their 2022 semi-final run
strengths_away: Dangerous pace and directness on the counter-attack via Isidor and Nazon
Compact defensive structure capable of frustrating stronger opponents
High motivation and underdog mentality returning to the World Cup after 52 years
team_style_home: Morocco deploy a compact 4-1-4-1 or 4-3-3, excelling in disciplined defensive organisation and rapid transitions, with Achraf Hakimi driving forward on the right and Brahim Diaz creating between the lines.
team_style_away: Haiti set up in a 4-2-3-1 or 4-4-2 defensive block, prioritising compactness and counter-attacking speed, looking to release Wilson Isidor and Duckens Nazon into space on the break.
recent_news: Morocco's World Cup 2026 preparations have been hit by key injuries ahead of their opening match against Brazil. Winger Abde Ezzalzouli suffered a knee injury in a friendly against Norway, while defender Nayef Aguerd remains sidelined following groin surgery in March.
context: Group C features five-time world champions Brazil alongside Africa's top-ranked side Morocco (7th in the world), returning giant-killers Scotland, and debutant Haiti — Morocco and Haiti meet on Matchday 3, with both teams needing points to secure or consolidate a knockout-stage berth.
